content.stem: Marcus practiced violin every day for two weeks before his recital. During the performance, he played beautifully without making any mistakes. What can you conclude about Marcus?

options.text: His practice helped him perform well
He is naturally talented at music
He was nervous during the recital
He had performed this piece before

content.explanation: The passage shows a clear connection between Marcus practicing every day and then performing well. This demonstrates that his preparation led to his successful performance.

metadata.cognitive_skill: causal reasoning and connecting evidence to conclusions
metadata.misconception_targeted: talent assumption and prior experience assumption rather than recognizing the role of practice

distractor_rationale.reasoning: Students might assume good performance means natural talent rather than recognizing the role of practice
Students might infer nervousness despite evidence of flawless performance
Students might assume previous experience rather than connecting current practice to performance
